intro: Transitioning from C to Java shifts development from a platform-dependent procedural model to a highly structured, object-oriented enterprise runtime environment on the JVM. This move replaces manual pointer management and compile-time hardware targets with cross-platform byte code deployment and automated garbage collection. It is ideal for upgrading low-level system utilities into secure, scalable, and highly concurrent enterprise services.

diffs.from: Statically typed, weakly enforced procedural types with no safety boundaries protecting memory layouts.
diffs.to: Statically typed, nominal class hierarchy heavily verified at compilation and execution runtime checkpoints.

diffs.from: Procedural syntax tracking variables, global methods, structure layouts, and independent pointer files.
diffs.to: Strict object-oriented layout where all functional code blocks, values, and methods must live inside class structures.

diffs.from: Manually handled via explicit heap definitions (malloc) and manual code clearing operations (free).
diffs.to: Automatically managed via automated JVM generational garbage collection sweeps running across background cycles.

diffs.from: No built-in language abstractions, relying entirely on platform-specific thread configurations (like pthreads).
diffs.to: Robust concurrent execution platforms, thread workers, synchronized lock monitors, and modern virtual threads.

diffs.from: Minimalist suite managing fundamental console streams, basic math, file inputs, and core memory streams.
diffs.to: Monolithic built-in runtime framework organizing international networking, secure cryptography, UI tools, and collections.

examples.title: Object Class Representation
examples.description: Refactoring low-level C structural maps into clean, structured Java classes with encapsulated data.

examples.title: Safe Exception Code Paths
examples.description: Converting manual C return-code status checks into clean, structured Java try-catch exception handling blocks.

tips.title: Encapsulate Logic in Classes
tips.body: Do not write detached global functions. Place all execution methods inside logical class boundaries to fit Java's object-oriented structure.

tips.title: Use Try-With-Resources
tips.body: Replace manual file closing routines with Java's try-with-resources statement to guarantee resource cleanup automatically.

tips.title: Swap Arrays for Collections
tips.body: Avoid tracking array sizes manually with separate variables. Use dynamic collections like ArrayList to manage list sizing automatically.

tips.title: Catch Explicit Exceptions
tips.body: Stop passing integer error flags back up your application calls. Use structured try-catch exception handling blocks to manage operational failures cleanly.
